item.name: Demo Scenarios
item.description:
Three realistic demo scenarios that showcase the RAG pipeline's two categories:

1. **LLM Fallback** — Generic question NOT in vector DB. Claude uses its trained knowledge.
2. **RAG + SQL Combo** — Generic definition (RAG/LLM) combined with a data query (SQL tool).
3. **Performance Report** — Full hybrid: SQL data + RAG context + LLM synthesis.

item.name: Scenario 1: LLM Fallback — Generic Definition
item.description:
Tests Category 2 (LLM Intelligence) fallback behavior.

The question asks for a general definition of 'enrollment' and its types.
This is NOT stored in the vector database — the business glossary only has
'Enrollment Rate' (a metric), not a general definition of enrollment itself.

Expected behavior:
- Agent calls rag_search_tool → finds partial match (Enrollment Rate glossary)
- RAG chunks provide some context but not a full definition
- Claude supplements with its own trained knowledge about enrollment types
  (open enrollment, automatic enrollment, opt-in, etc.)
- Final answer blends RAG context + LLM's general knowledge

This demonstrates that Claude can fall back on its trained model when the
vector database doesn't have an exact match — a key advantage of Category 2.

item.name: 1a. Ask: General definition of enrollment (via /ask — agent decides)
1b. Search: Check what RAG actually has about enrollment (raw search)
Scenario 2: RAG + SQL — Definition + Data Query
item.description:
Tests both categories working together on a compound question.

Part A asks for a definition (RAG search + LLM fallback).
Part B asks which campaign had the maximum enrollments (SQL query).
Part C asks both in a single question — the agent must call BOTH tools.

Expected behavior for Part C:
- Agent recognizes this is a compound question
- Calls rag_search_tool for the 'enrollment' definition (Steps 6-8)
- Calls sql_query_tool to find max enrollments (Steps 9-11)
- Combines both results into a single coherent answer

This demonstrates the agent's ability to orchestrate multiple tools
and merge Category 1 (retrieval) with Category 2 (generation).

item.name: 2a. Ask: What is enrollment? (definition via agent)
2b. Ask SQL: Which campaign had maximum enrollments? (data query)
2c. Ask: Combined — definition + data in one question (agent uses both tools)
Scenario 3: Campaign Performance Report
item.description:
Tests the full hybrid flow using the performance_summary_tool.

CMP-003 (Spring Dining Deal) is chosen because it demonstrates the most
interesting story: highest redemption rate (74%), student-focused targeting,
smallest budget ($100K) yet highest ROI (210%), mobile-dominant channel.

Expected behavior:
- Agent calls performance_summary_tool with campaign_id='CMP-003'
- Tool executes 3 SQL queries against SQLite:
  1. Monthly performance metrics joined with campaign info
  2. Aggregate enrollment count
  3. Aggregate redemption count + total amount
- Tool calls rag_search_tool for qualitative context (Steps 6-8)
- All data assembled into augmented prompt (Step 9)
- Sent to Claude for synthesis (Step 10)
- Claude returns a 3-4 paragraph business report (Step 11)

This is the most complex flow — it exercises both categories fully:
- Category 1: RAG retrieval of campaign description + performance summary
- Category 2: SQL execution, prompt assembly, LLM narrative generation

item.name: 3a. Get: Campaign details for CMP-003 (raw data)
3b. Search: RAG context for CMP-003 (what the knowledge base knows)
3c. Ask SQL: Monthly performance trend for CMP-003 (raw SQL data)
3d. Get Summary: Full AI-generated performance report for CMP-003
3e. Ask: Compare CMP-003 vs CMP-004 — best ROI on budget (complex query)
